paper.title_en: Reaching diffraction-limited localization with coherent PTAs
paper.abstract_en: Current pulsar timing array (PTA) analyses are phase incoherent and thus to not take full advantage of pulsar distance information, thereby missing out on improved angular resolution and on a potential factor-of-two gain in detection sensitivity for individual gravitational-wave (GW) sources. In this work, we investigate the impact of precise pulsar distance measurements on angular resolution as an extension to previous work measuring the angular resolution of a dense isotropic PTA [D. L. Jow , How many pixels are there in a polarized pulsar timing array map?, Phys. Rev. D 113, 043034 (2026)]. We present a coherent map-making technique that utilizes precise pulsar distance measurements to reach a diffraction-limited resolution of an individual source: <math display="inline"><mi>δ</mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mrow><mi>diff</mi></mrow></msub><mo>×</mo><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mi>SNR</mi><mo stretchy="false">)</mo><mo>≈</mo><mn>1</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext> </mtext><mi>arcmin</mi></math>, where <math display="inline"><mi>δ</mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mrow><mi>diff</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>λ</mi><mi>GW</mi></msub><mo>/</mo><mi>r</mi></math> is the diffractive angle and SNR refers to the detection strength of the source. With this level of angular resolution, identifying an electromagnetic counterpart may become feasible, enabling multimessenger follow-up. We show that for SNR = 10, which may be the current sensitivity level using a coherent analysis, the diffraction limit is reached with approximately ten equidistant pulsars with distances of about 300 parsecs. Moreover, angular resolution scales sharply with the number of known pulsar distances as <math display="inline"><mo>∼</mo><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mi>SNR</mi><msup><mo stretchy="false">)</mo><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mrow><mi>dist</mi></mrow></msub><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Thus, each additional pulsar with high signal-to-noise timing and precise distance measurement can improve PTA resolution by an order of magnitude. The distance to the best-timed millisecond pulsar (PSR <math display="inline"><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mn>0437</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>4715</mn></math>) is already constrained to sub-parsec levels. We argue, therefore, that a coherent analysis of PTA data, fully incorporating pulsar distance information, is timely.

decision.selection_reason_zh: 该论文提出了一种利用精确脉冲星距离测量实现衍射极限角分辨率的相干PTA分析方法，并展示了其对于引力波源定位和多信使天文学的潜力。方法新颖，分析严谨，对于PTA领域具有重要价值。
decision.title_zh: 使用相干脉冲星计时阵列达到衍射极限定位
decision.abstract_zh: 目前的脉冲星计时阵列（PTA）分析是相位不相干的，因此没有充分利用脉冲星距离信息，从而错过了提高角分辨率以及对于单个引力波（GW）源在探测灵敏度上潜在的两倍增益。在这项工作中，我们研究了精确脉冲星距离测量对角分辨率的影响，作为对先前测量密集各向同性PTA角分辨率工作的扩展[D. L. Jow等人，极化脉冲星计时阵列图中有多少像素？，Phys. Rev. D 113, 043034 (2026)]。我们提出了一种相干绘图技术，利用精确的脉冲星距离测量来达到单个源的衍射极限分辨率：<math display="inline"><mi>δ</mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mrow><mi>diff</mi></mrow></msub><mo>×</mo><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mi>SNR</mi><mo stretchy="false">)</mo><mo>≈</mo><mn>1</mn><mtext> </mtext><mtext> </mtext><mi>arcmin</mi></math>，其中<math display="inline"><mi>δ</mi><msub><mi>θ</mi><mrow><mi>diff</mi></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><msub><mi>λ</mi><mi>GW</mi></msub><mo>/</mo><mi>r</mi></math>是衍射角，SNR指的是源的探测强度。在这个角分辨率水平下，识别电磁对应体可能变得可行，从而实现多信使后随观测。我们表明，对于SNR = 10（这可能是使用相干分析目前的灵敏度水平），使用大约十个距离约为300秒差距的等距脉冲星即可达到衍射极限。此外，角分辨率随着已知脉冲星距离数量的增加而急剧提高，为<math display="inline"><mo>∼</mo><mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>/</mo><mi>SNR</mi><msup><mo stretchy="false">)</mo><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mrow><mi>dist</mi></mrow></msub><mo>/</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>。因此，每个额外具有高信噪比计时和精确距离测量的脉冲星都可以将PTA分辨率提高一个数量级。最佳计时毫秒脉冲星（PSR <math display="inline"><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi><mn>0437</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>4715</mn></math>）的距离已被限制在亚秒差距水平。因此，我们认为，完全纳入脉冲星距离信息的PTA数据相干分析是及时的。
